name: National Book Festival
description: Library of Congress flagship festival at the Walter E. Washington Convention Center; free, one-day celebration of reading.

name: LA Times Festival of Books
description: The nation's largest public book festival, held on the USC campus over two days with hundreds of authors and exhibitors.

name: Miami Book Fair
description: Eight-day fair at Miami Dade College drawing 550+ authors from 35+ countries; one of the most important literary events in the Americas.

name: Texas Book Festival
description: Annual two-day festival around the Texas State Capitol featuring 250+ authors, a street fair, and a Saturday Lit Crawl.

name: Southern Festival of Books
description: Free two-day festival at Bicentennial Mall and the Tennessee State Museum celebrating Southern literature and authors.

name: Brooklyn Book Festival
description: New York City's largest free literary event, spanning nine days with 500+ authors, the East Coast's biggest book market, and five boroughs.

name: Tucson Festival of Books
description: Free two-day festival on the University of Arizona campus with 300+ authors, science exhibitions, and family programming.

name: Printers Row Lit Fest
description: Free outdoor book fair in Chicago's historic Printers Row neighborhood; the Midwest's premier street literary festival.

name: Savannah Book Festival
description: Four-day festival in historic downtown Savannah with 40+ authors presenting at venues surrounding Telfair and Chippewa Squares.

name: Baltimore Book Festival
description: Free two-day festival in Baltimore's Waverly neighborhood featuring 80+ authors, six stages, literary panels, and youth programming.

name: Decatur Book Festival
description: Free two-day festival in downtown Decatur with 100+ national and local authors, a street fair, and cooking demonstrations.

name: Virginia Festival of the Book
description: Four-day literary festival at the University of Virginia celebrating authors, ideas, and the literary arts across Charlottesville.

name: ThrillerFest
description: Annual conference of the International Thriller Writers at the New York Hilton Midtown; workshops, panels, and the ITW Award ceremony.

name: Bouchercon World Mystery Convention
description: The world's largest mystery fan convention, rotating cities annually; the 2025 edition returns to New Orleans in September.
recurringPattern: Annual – rotating city, typically late August/September

name: Portland Book Festival
description: Annual one-day festival (formerly Wordstock) across ten stages in Portland's South Park Blocks, presented by Literary Arts.

name: Litquake / Lit Crawl SF
description: SF's two-week literary festival culminating in Lit Crawl, the world's largest free pop-up lit event with 250+ authors roaming the Mission District.

name: Key West Literary Seminar
description: Intimate four-day seminar exploring a single literary theme each year, drawing writers and readers to the Key West Amphitheater.

name: Sun Valley Writers' Conference
description: Prestigious three-day gathering of leading authors and thinkers at the Sun Valley Resort Pavilion in the Idaho mountains.

name: Nantucket Book Festival
description: Four-day island literary festival with free public events featuring bestselling and Pulitzer Prize-winning authors each June.

name: New Orleans Book Festival at Tulane
description: Free four-day festival on Tulane University's uptown campus with 90+ panels, culinary symposia, and family programming.

name: Boston Book Festival
description: Free one-day festival in Copley Square with 200+ authors presenting across the Boston Public Library and surrounding historic churches.

name: Louisville Book Festival
description: Free one-day festival at the Kentucky International Convention Center with 100+ authors celebrating reading and storytelling.

name: PEN World Voices Festival
description: PEN America's international literary festival celebrating writers from 35+ countries with events in Manhattan's Greenwich Village.
recurringPattern: Annual – late April to early May

name: Twin Cities Book Festival
description: Rain Taxi's annual all-day book fair at Union Depot with 130+ publishers, literary organizations, and author events for all ages.

name: Kentucky Book Festival
description: Free all-day festival at Joseph-Beth Booksellers in Lexington with 150+ authors celebrating books and literacy in the Commonwealth.

name: Harlem Book Fair
description: The nation's largest African American book fair on 135th Street in Harlem, celebrating Black literature, culture, and intellectual life each July.

name: Bronx Book Fair
description: Annual one-day celebration of books and literacy at Hostos Community College in the South Bronx, with local and national authors, free admission.

name: Queens Book Festival
description: Free annual festival in Jamaica, Queens celebrating the borough's diverse literary voices with author readings, panels, and family programming.

name: NYC Poetry Festival
description: Free two-day outdoor poetry festival on Governors Island featuring 200+ poets, publishers, and literary magazines in a stunning harbor setting.

name: Chautauqua Literary Arts Festival
description: Summer-long literary arts programming at the historic Chautauqua Institution, including author readings, the Chautauqua Prize, and writing workshops.

name: Vermont Book Festival
description: Multi-day festival celebrating Vermont authors and the state's vibrant literary culture with readings, panels, and a bustling book fair in Burlington.

name: Bread Loaf Writers' Conference
description: America's oldest and most prestigious writers' conference, held each August in the Green Mountains; founded by Robert Frost in 1926 at Middlebury College.

name: New Hampshire Literary Festival
description: Annual two-day celebration of New Hampshire authors and literature, with panel discussions, readings, and a book fair at Concord venues.

name: Free Library Literary Festival
description: Philadelphia's premier literary gathering hosted by the Free Library, featuring award-winning authors across fiction, nonfiction, poetry, and children's literature.

name: Delaware Book Festival
description: Free annual festival at Legislative Mall in Dover with Delaware authors and national guests, presented by the Delaware Division of Libraries.

name: Garden State Teen Book Festival
description: New Jersey's largest literary event for teens and young adults, bringing 50+ YA and middle-grade authors to Garden State Arts Center.

name: Dodge Poetry Festival
description: The largest poetry event in North America, held biennially at NJPAC in Newark with 100+ poets, 18,000+ attendees, and four days of readings and workshops.

name: Malice Domestic
description: Annual mystery fan convention in suburban DC celebrating the traditional mystery, honoring the Agatha Awards, and connecting readers with authors.

name: Split This Rock Poetry Festival
description: Biennial festival in Washington, DC celebrating socially engaged poetry; features 200+ poets, workshops, and the Poem of the Week gallery.

name: Hurston/Wright Writers Week
description: Intensive summer writing workshop and awards celebration for Black writers of African descent, honoring the legacy of Zora Neale Hurston and Richard Wright.

name: Tennessee Williams/New Orleans Literary Festival
description: Five-day festival in the French Quarter honoring the playwright's legacy with author readings, a Stanley and Stella shouting contest, and literary walking tours.

name: Mississippi Book Festival
description: Free one-day literary lawn party on the grounds of the Mississippi State Capitol celebrating the state's rich storytelling tradition.

name: Oxford Conference for the Book
description: Three-day academic and popular literary conference at the University of Mississippi exploring books, publishing, and Southern literature.

name: Arkansas Book Festival
description: Free one-day festival at the Arkansas Studies Institute and Fulbright Library celebrating Arkansas authors and readers across all genres.

name: Alabama Book Festival
description: Free one-day outdoor festival on the grounds of the Old Alabama Town complex featuring 70+ authors, booksellers, and a children's programming area.

name: North Carolina Literary Festival
description: Biennial statewide celebration of North Carolina writers hosted by UNC-Chapel Hill, featuring readings, workshops, and a book fair across the university campus.

name: South Carolina Book Festival
description: Free two-day festival at the Columbia Metropolitan Convention Center with 100+ authors, celebrity chefs, and the SC Fiction Project readings.

name: Tallahassee Book Festival
description: Free one-day outdoor festival on the historic Kleman Plaza featuring Florida authors, storytelling for children, poetry slams, and independent booksellers.

name: Jacksonville Book Festival
description: Multi-day literary celebration in downtown Jacksonville featuring panel discussions, author signings, and events at the Jacksonville Main Library.

name: Pensacola Book Festival
description: Annual free festival at the Pensacola Bay Center celebrating Gulf Coast authors and literacy with readings, booksellers, and family programming.

name: West Virginia Book Festival
description: Three-day festival at the Charleston Civic Center celebrating Appalachian and national literature with author readings, a book sale, and children's events.

name: Sewanee Writers' Conference
description: Highly selective 12-day conference at the University of the South in Sewanee, TN, offering workshops in fiction, poetry, and playwriting with celebrated faculty.

name: Thomas Wolfe Festival
description: Annual October festival in Asheville honoring native son Thomas Wolfe with readings at the Thomas Wolfe Memorial, author events, and literary tours.

name: Flannery O'Connor Festival
description: Annual symposium at Georgia College celebrating Flannery O'Connor's work and legacy, with scholarly panels, readings, and tours of her Andalusia Farm.

name: Carolina Mountains Literary Festival
description: Intimate three-day literary festival in the Blue Ridge Mountains of western NC, featuring 30+ authors in a beautiful small-town Appalachian setting.

name: Appalachian Writers' Workshop
description: Week-long writing workshop and literary celebration at Hindman Settlement School in the Kentucky mountains, one of the oldest regional Appalachian literary gatherings.

name: Wisconsin Book Festival
description: Five-day free literary festival hosted by the Madison Public Library featuring 100+ authors across fiction, memoir, poetry, and children's literature.

name: Ohioana Book Festival
description: Free one-day celebration of Ohio authors and books at the Ohioana Library in Columbus, presenting the Ohioana Book Awards and featuring 100+ Ohio writers.

name: Iowa City Book Festival
description: Annual fall festival in the UNESCO City of Literature celebrating the written word with author events across Iowa City's independent bookshops and cultural venues.

name: Ann Arbor Book Festival
description: Annual literary celebration in Ann Arbor featuring author readings and a streetfair of independent publishers and booksellers in the heart of this literary college town.

name: Festival of Faith & Writing
description: Biennial gathering at Calvin University exploring the intersection of literature and faith with 100+ authors including Pulitzer and National Book Award winners.

name: Kenyon Review Writers Workshop
description: Intensive summer workshop at Kenyon College for adult writers of poetry, fiction, and nonfiction, with faculty drawn from the Kenyon Review editorial community.

name: South Dakota Festival of Books
description: Annual three-day free literary festival presented by South Dakota Humanities Council at South Dakota State University with 50+ authors and family programming.

name: Kansas Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival at the Kansas State Capitol and Topeka & Shawnee County Public Library celebrating Kansas authors and literary culture.

name: Southern Kentucky Book Fest
description: Annual free book festival at the Knicely Conference Center in Bowling Green, featuring 100+ authors and presented by the Warren County Public Library.

name: Montana Festival of the Book
description: Annual four-day fall festival celebrating Montana's rich literary tradition in the college-town atmosphere of Missoula, with readings, panels, and bookstore events.

name: San Antonio Book Festival
description: Free one-day festival at the Central Library and Travis Park with 150+ authors celebrating San Antonio's diverse literary culture and Texas storytelling.

name: North Texas Book Festival
description: Annual free fall festival at Denton's Emily Fowler Central Library celebrating North Texas authors, independent publishers, and family reading.

name: El Paso Book Festival
description: Bilingual literary celebration on the US-Mexico border showcasing Borderlands authors in English and Spanish, with a focus on border culture and identity.

name: Arizona Book Festival
description: Annual free festival at Burton Barr Central Library celebrating Arizona authors and readers with author talks, panels, and a children's literacy fair.

name: Albuquerque Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival celebrating the diverse literary voices of New Mexico and the Southwest, held at the Albuquerque Convention Center.

name: Taos Summer Writers' Conference
description: Week-long intensive writing conference in the mountains of northern New Mexico at the University of New Mexico Taos campus; fiction, poetry, and creative nonfiction.

name: Utah Humanities Book Festival
description: Annual statewide literary celebration presented by Utah Humanities, with author events at libraries and cultural venues across Salt Lake City and Utah.

name: Aspen Summer Words Literary Festival
description: Annual five-day writing retreat and literary festival presented by Aspen Words in the Rocky Mountains, with readings, craft seminars, and the Aspen Words Literary Prize.

name: Pikes Peak Writers Conference
description: Annual three-day conference for genre fiction writers in Colorado Springs featuring agents, editors, and bestselling authors in a workshop-intensive setting.

name: Nevada Humanities Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival presented by Nevada Humanities at the National Automobile Museum in Reno celebrating Nevada authors and the literary arts.

name: Bay Area Book Festival
description: Free two-day festival in downtown Berkeley bringing together 300+ authors, 100+ exhibitors, and 30,000+ book lovers for the West Coast's premier literary celebration.

name: San Diego Festival of Books
description: Annual free literary festival celebrating San Diego authors and the region's diverse reading culture at the San Diego Library and Balboa Park venues.

name: Sacramento Book Fair
description: Annual gathering of independent booksellers, small presses, and literary organizations celebrating books and reading culture in California's capital.

name: Santa Barbara Book Festival
description: Annual celebration of books and ideas at Santa Barbara's independent bookshops, the Santa Barbara Public Library, and the historic Lobero Theatre.

name: Willamette Writers Conference
description: One of the largest writer's conferences on the West Coast, held each August in Portland with 100+ sessions, an agent/editor pitch room, and keynote authors.

name: Tin House Summer Workshop
description: Prestigious week-long workshop at Reed College with fiction, poetry, and nonfiction tracks; one of the most competitive summer literary programs in the country.

name: Seattle Book Festival
description: Annual literary event hosted by Hugo House across Seattle venues featuring Northwest authors, publisher showcases, and an inclusive celebration of Seattle's reading culture.

name: Centrum Writers' Conference
description: Week-long summer conference at Centrum's Fort Worden State Park on the Olympic Peninsula; intimate workshops in fiction, poetry, and creative nonfiction since 1974.

name: Clarion West Writers Workshop
description: Intensive six-week speculative fiction workshop in Seattle's Capitol Hill, one of the most competitive SF/fantasy writing programs in the world.

name: Locus Awards Weekend
description: Annual science fiction and fantasy awards weekend presenting the Locus Awards, voted on by readers of Locus Magazine; includes panels, readings, and a gala ceremony.

name: Alaska Book Festival
description: Annual celebration of Alaskan literature and literary culture at the Anchorage Museum, featuring Alaska Native storytellers, adventure writers, and nature authors.

name: Hawaii Book & Music Festival
description: Annual free outdoor festival on the grounds of Honolulu Hale celebrating Pacific Island literature, local authors, and Hawaii's unique multicultural storytelling traditions.

name: Lambda Literary Awards
description: Annual awards gala and literary celebration honoring the best LGBTQ+ books of the year, presented by Lambda Literary in New York City.

name: Edgar Awards Weekend
description: Annual Mystery Writers of America awards banquet presenting the Edgar Allan Poe Awards for best mystery fiction, nonfiction, and children's mystery writing.

name: SCBWI Summer Conference
description: The Society of Children's Book Writers and Illustrators' flagship annual conference in LA with agents, editors, and 1,000+ children's book creators.

name: AWP Conference & Bookfair
description: The largest literary conference in North America with 12,000+ attendees, 2,000+ events, and a massive bookfair of literary presses; rotates to major US cities annually.

name: Left Coast Crime
description: Annual mystery fan convention rotating among West Coast cities, celebrating crime fiction with author panels, awards, and the Lefty Award ceremony.
recurringPattern: Annual – March/April, rotating West Coast cities

name: World Fantasy Convention
description: Annual fantasy and horror literature convention rotating US and international cities, presenting the World Fantasy Awards and gathering the genre's top authors and editors.

name: Nebula Awards Conference
description: Annual Science Fiction and Fantasy Writers of America conference presenting the Nebula Awards, rotating among US cities; includes craft panels and an awards banquet.

name: Romance Writers of America Conference
description: Annual conference of romance writers presenting the RITA Awards and Golden Heart Awards, with agent and editor appointments and craft workshops.

name: Words & Music: A Literary Feast in New Orleans
description: Faulkner Society's annual November festival in the French Quarter pairing literature with New Orleans food and music; features emerging and established fiction writers.

name: Houston Poetry Fest
description: Annual two-day festival at Evelyn's Park Conservancy celebrating poetry with 100+ poets, open-mic events, slam competitions, and readings for all ages.

name: Nebraska Book Festival
description: Annual literary celebration presented by Nebraska Humanities at the Nebraska State Capitol celebrating Nebraska authors and the state's literary heritage.

name: North Dakota Humanities Book Festival
description: Annual literary event presented by North Dakota Humanities celebrating Great Plains authors, indigenous storytellers, and regional literary voices.

name: Heartland Book Festival
description: Annual celebration of authors and books in Kansas City, featuring Midwest writers and national guests at the Kansas City Public Library's central branch.

name: VONA/Voices Writing Workshop
description: Summer writing workshop for writers of color at the University of Miami, offering intensive fiction, poetry, creative nonfiction, and screenwriting tracks.

name: Suncoast Literary Festival
description: Annual multi-day literary celebration on Florida's Gulf Coast featuring national bestselling authors, local Florida writers, and programming at Sarasota venues.

name: Wordplay – A Festival of Minnesota Lit
description: Annual one-day free literary festival at Open Book in Minneapolis celebrating Minnesota authors, the Minnesota Book Awards, and the state's vibrant literary scene.

name: VCFA Symposium
description: Annual public literary symposium hosted by Vermont College of Fine Arts MFA programs, featuring VCFA faculty and alumni who are leading writers in their fields.
recurringPattern: Annual – summer residency, January and July

name: Rocky Mountain Fiction Writers Colorado Gold
description: Annual fall conference for fiction writers in Denver featuring agents, editors, and bestselling authors; one of the top regional writing conferences in the Mountain West.

name: Richmond Book Arts Festival
description: Annual celebration of book arts, handmade books, and literary culture at the Valentine Museum in Richmond, with printmakers, bookbinders, and authors.

name: Nashville Book Festival
description: Annual free one-day event at the Nashville Public Library celebrating books and reading with Nashville authors, national guests, and family programming.

name: Memphis Book Fair
description: Annual literary celebration in Memphis featuring local and national authors, small presses, and literary organizations celebrating the Mississippi Delta's storytelling heritage.

name: Atlanta Book Fair at MOCA GA
description: Annual free book fair at the Museum of Contemporary Art of Georgia celebrating Atlanta's independent publishers, small presses, and literary community.

name: Detroit Literary Festival
description: Annual celebration of Detroit's literary history and contemporary authors, featuring readings at the Detroit Public Library and venues across Midtown.

name: Columbus Book Festival
description: Annual literary celebration at COSI and the Columbus Metropolitan Library celebrating books, authors, and Ohio's reading community.

name: Pittsburgh Contemporary Writers Series
description: Annual literary celebration at Carnegie Mellon University and across Pittsburgh venues featuring national and regional authors in fiction, poetry, and nonfiction.

name: Seattle Lit Crawl
description: Annual one-night literary pub crawl through Seattle's Capitol Hill neighborhood with dozens of readings in bars, restaurants, and venues; part of the Lit Crawl network.

name: Chicago Humanities Festival
description: Annual multi-week public festival presenting the world's leading authors, thinkers, and artists at venues across Chicago including Millennium Park and the Chicago Cultural Center.

name: O, Miami Poetry Festival
description: Annual month-long April celebration placing a poem in front of every Miami-Dade County resident, with 200+ events in parks, galleries, buses, and public spaces.

name: San Diego Writers Festival
description: Annual literary celebration bringing together San Diego's diverse writing community with readings, workshops, and author talks across the city's cultural venues.

name: New Mexico Book Fiesta
description: Annual free outdoor literary festival on the Santa Fe Plaza celebrating New Mexico's rich literary tradition, Southwest authors, and bilingual storytelling.

name: Shreveport Book Festival
description: Annual literary festival in northwest Louisiana at the Barnwell Garden and Art Center celebrating regional Southern authors and Louisiana's storytelling traditions.

name: Oxford Literary Festival at Ole Miss
description: Annual spring literary weekend in Oxford, MS — Faulkner's hometown — celebrating Southern writing with author talks, square strolling, and Square Books events.

name: Boulder Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival at the Boulder Public Library and downtown venues celebrating Colorado authors and the Front Range's vibrant literary community.

name: Lit Fest DC
description: Annual free literary festival in Washington, DC celebrating the capital's diverse literary community with author readings at libraries and cultural venues across the city.

name: Phoenix Literature Festival
description: Annual celebration of Arizona literary arts at Phoenix Center for the Arts, featuring diverse local and national authors across poetry, fiction, and creative nonfiction.

name: Mission Creek Festival
description: Annual spring festival in Iowa City blending literature, music, and film across bars, bookstores, and cultural venues in the heart of this UNESCO City of Literature.

name: South Texas Literary Festival
description: Annual celebration of South Texas and border-region authors at Texas A&M University-Corpus Christi, featuring bilingual literature and Gulf Coast storytelling.

name: Spokane Is Reading
description: Annual citywide reading festival in Spokane presenting author events at the Spokane Public Library, bookshops, and cultural venues across the Inland Northwest.

name: Oklahoma Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival at the State Capitol complex celebrating Oklahoma authors and literacy with readings, workshops, and a book signing fair.

name: Fayetteville Roots Festival
description: Annual roots music and literary arts festival in Fayetteville pairing Southern authors and storytellers with the Ozark mountain music tradition.

name: Tucson Book Arts Fair
description: Annual celebration of handmade books, letterpress printing, and the book arts at the University of Arizona Center for Creative Photography.

name: Black Hills Literary Festival
description: Annual literary gathering in historic Deadwood, South Dakota celebrating Western American literature, Native American storytelling, and frontier literary traditions.

name: Prairie Lights Literary Series
description: Year-round live readings series at Prairie Lights Books, one of America's great independent bookstores; a landmark literary institution in the Iowa Writers' Workshop community.

name: Word Festival
description: Annual three-day literary festival in the pastoral village of Cooperstown, NY featuring award-winning authors in an intimate setting alongside the Baseball Hall of Fame.

name: Wichita Literary Festival
description: Annual free literary celebration at Wichita Public Library featuring Kansas authors, national guests, and programming for readers of all ages across the city.

name: Vermont Humanities Festival
description: Annual statewide humanities celebration hosted by Vermont Humanities with author talks, book discussions, and literary events across Vermont communities.

name: Pasadena Literary Festival
description: Annual literary celebration at Vroman's Bookstore and Pasadena Central Library, the longest-running independent bookstore event in Southern California.

name: Seattle Poetry Festival
description: Annual multi-day poetry celebration in Seattle featuring Northwest poets and national voices at venues across the city, celebrating Seattle's status as a poetry capital.

name: Idaho Book Festival
description: Annual free literary celebration at the Boise Centre and Boise Public Library featuring Idaho authors and national writers celebrating the Gem State's literary heritage.

name: Roanoke Regional Book Festival
description: Annual literary celebration in Virginia's Blue Ridge mountain city featuring Appalachian authors and regional writers at the Taubman Museum of Art.

name: St. Pete Book Festival
description: Annual free two-day festival in downtown St. Petersburg celebrating Florida and national authors with readings at Williams Park and literary venues across the city.

name: Orlando Book Festival
description: Annual literary event at the Orange County Library System celebrating Central Florida authors and offering free author talks, workshops, and a book fair.

name: Greater Lehigh Valley Book Festival
description: Annual free book festival at Steel Stacks in Bethlehem celebrating Pennsylvania authors and the written word in the historic Lehigh Valley.

name: San Francisco Literary Festival
description: Annual celebration of San Francisco's legendary literary culture at venues from North Beach to the Mission, honoring the city's Beat Generation legacy and contemporary voices.

name: Cape Cod Literary Festival
description: Annual summer literary gathering on Cape Cod featuring award-winning authors in the historic town of Chatham, celebrating New England storytelling and the sea.

name: Los Angeles Literary Festival
description: Annual fall celebration of LA's diverse literary voices at the Hammer Museum and Westside venues, separate from the spring Times Festival of Books.

name: Chicago International Literature Festival
description: Annual festival celebrating literature in translation and the diverse immigrant and international voices that make Chicago one of America's most literary cities.

name: Denver Art Book Fair
description: Annual gathering of independent publishers, zine makers, and art book creators at the Denver Art Museum celebrating artist books and literary art objects.

name: Connecticut Book Awards & Festival
description: Annual celebration of Connecticut authors presenting the Connecticut Book Awards with readings, panels, and a book fair at the Connecticut State Library in Hartford.

name: Maine Festival of the Book
description: Annual literary celebration on the Maine coast featuring Maine authors, small presses, and national voices at the Portland Public Library and Congress Street venues.

name: Rhode Island Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival at the Providence Public Library and RISD Museum celebrating Rhode Island authors and New England literary culture.

name: Indiana Humanities Bookfest
description: Annual free literary event at the Indiana State Library celebrating Indiana authors and the humanities with readings, author talks, and a community book discussion.

name: Wyoming Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival presented by Wyoming Humanities at the Wyoming State Library, celebrating Western American literature and Wyoming's storied frontier heritage.
